name: New York Dietitian Certification
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition

address.description:
New York State Education Department
Office of the Professions
Dietetics and Nutrition Unit
89 Washington Avenue
Albany, NY 12234-1000

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the NYSED.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Certification Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Child Abuse Training Certificate
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate of completion for the mandatory NYS child abuse identification coursework.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New York dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is $294, which covers the NYSED's $115 application fee and the $179 fee for your first three-year registration period.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a master's degree to become a CDN in New York?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. As of January 1, 2024, a minimum of a master's degree is required by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to sit for the national RDN exam, which serves as the exam basis for NY state certification.

mainEntity.name: Is continuing education required to renew a New York CDN license?

acceptedAnswer.text: The NYSED does not mandate additional state-level continuing education for renewal, but you must maintain your active CDR registration, which requires completing 75 hours of continuing professional education every 5 years.

mainEntity.name: Does New York have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New York?
Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
